

```{r}
#===============================================================================
# Population Structure Analysis for Intermediate Wheatgrass (Kernza)
# Author: Elizabeth [Last Name]
# Affiliation: Department of Tropical Plant and Soil Sciences, 
#              University of Hawaii at Manoa
# Date: [Date]
#
# Description: Principal components analysis, hierarchical clustering, and 
#              spatial structure analysis of 341 Thinopyrum intermedium accessions
#              using 17,797 SNPs from genotyping-by-sequencing.
#
# Reference: Methods based on Zheng et al. (2012), Lê et al. (2008), 
#            and Caye et al. (2018)
#===============================================================================

#===============================================================================
# 1. SETUP AND LIBRARY LOADING
#===============================================================================

# Set working directory - MODIFY THIS PATH FOR YOUR SYSTEM
setwd("path/to/your/data")

# Load required packages
library(SNPRelate)      # v1.28.0 - PCA and LD pruning
library(FactoMineR)     # v2.8 - HCPC clustering
library(factoextra)     # Visualization of multivariate analyses
library(ggplot2)        # Publication-quality graphics
library(dplyr)          # Data manipulation
library(sf)             # Spatial data handling
library(rnaturalearth)  # World map data
library(tess3r)         # v1.1.0 - Spatial population structure
library(rrBLUP)         # Kinship matrix calculation

# Set random seed for reproducibility
set.seed(1000)

#===============================================================================
# 2. DATA IMPORT AND SNP FILTERING
#===============================================================================

# Import VCF file and convert to GDS format
vcf.fn <- "IWG_PI.vcf"
snpgdsVCF2GDS(vcf.fn, "IWG.gds", method = "copy.num.of.ref")

# Open GDS file and examine summary
genofile <- snpgdsOpen("IWG.gds")
snpgdsSummary("IWG.gds")

# Filter SNPs: MAF > 0.005 and missing data < 5%
# Initial markers: 17,797 SNPs
# Post-filtering: 5,449 SNPs
snp.filter <- snpgdsLDpruning(genofile, 
                               maf = 0.005,
                               missing.rate = 0.05,
                               ld.threshold = 0.2,
                               autosome.only = FALSE,
                               slide.max.bp = 500000,  # 500-kb sliding window
                               slide.max.n = NA)

# Extract filtered SNP IDs
snpset.id <- unlist(snp.filter)
cat("Number of SNPs after LD pruning:", length(snpset.id), "\n")
# Expected output: 4,703 independent markers

#===============================================================================
# 3. PRINCIPAL COMPONENTS ANALYSIS
#===============================================================================

# Perform PCA on LD-pruned SNPs
pca <- snpgdsPCA(genofile, 
                 snp.id = snpset.id, 
                 num.thread = 2,
                 autosome.only = FALSE)

# Calculate variance explained by each PC
pc.percent <- pca$varprop * 100
cat("\nVariance explained by first 5 PCs:\n")
print(round(pc.percent[1:5], 2))
# PC1: 13.7%, PC2: 2.3%, PC3-5: ~1-2% each (approximate 20% cumulative)

# Create dataframe with sample IDs and first 5 PCs
pca_data <- data.frame(
  sample.id = pca$sample.id,
  EV1 = pca$eigenvect[, 1],
  EV2 = pca$eigenvect[, 2],
  EV3 = pca$eigenvect[, 3],
  EV4 = pca$eigenvect[, 4],
  EV5 = pca$eigenvect[, 5],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Export PCA results
write.csv(pca_data, "IWG_pca_eigenvectors.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Close GDS file
snpgdsClose(genofile)

#===============================================================================
# 4. HIERARCHICAL CLUSTERING ON PRINCIPAL COMPONENTS (HCPC)
#===============================================================================

# Prepare data for HCPC (remove sample ID column)
pca_matrix <- pca_data[, 2:6]
rownames(pca_matrix) <- pca_data$sample.id

# Perform PCA using FactoMineR (required for HCPC)
res.pca <- PCA(pca_matrix, graph = FALSE)

# Generate scree plot
scree_plot <- fviz_screeplot(res.pca, 
                              addlabels = TRUE, 
                              ylim = c(0, 50),
                              main = "Scree Plot of Principal Components")
ggsave("PCA_scree_plot.pdf", scree_plot, width = 8, height = 6)

# Perform HCPC with automatic cluster selection
# Uses Ward's hierarchical clustering with inertia gain criterion
res.hcpc <- HCPC(res.pca, 
                 graph = FALSE,
                 nb.clust = -1)  # Automatic determination of optimal K

# Extract cluster assignments
# Optimal K = 6 clusters (n = 24, 12, 27, 28, 169, 61)
cat("\nNumber of clusters identified:", max(res.hcpc$data.clust$clust), "\n")
cat("Cluster sizes:\n")
print(table(res.hcpc$data.clust$clust))

# Create dendrogram with cluster colors
dendrogram <- fviz_dend(res.hcpc, 
                        cex = 0.5,
                        palette = "jco",
                        rect = TRUE,
                        rect_fill = TRUE,
                        rect_border = "jco",
                        labels_track_height = 0.8,
                        main = "Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram (n = 341)")

ggsave("HCPC_dendrogram.pdf", dendrogram, width = 12, height = 8)

# Export cluster assignments
pca_with_clusters <- res.hcpc$data.clust
pca_with_clusters$sample.id <- rownames(pca_with_clusters)
write.csv(pca_with_clusters, "IWG_pca_with_clusters.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```


```{r}
#===============================================================================
# 5. VISUALIZATION: 2D PCA PLOT
#===============================================================================

# Prepare data
plot_data <- pca_with_clusters
plot_data$clust <- as.factor(plot_data$clust)
```


```{r}
# Create publication-quality PCA plot with actual variance values
pca_plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = EV1, y = EV2, color = clust, fill = clust)) +
  stat_ellipse(type = "t", 
               geom = "polygon", 
               alpha = 0.15, 
               show.legend = FALSE,
               level = 0.95) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(
  values = c("1" = "red", "2" = "green", "3" = "blue",
             "4" = "purple", "5" = "orange", "6" = "yellow"),
  name = "Genetic Cluster"
) +
scale_fill_manual(
  values = c("1" = "red", "2" = "green", "3" = "blue",
             "4" = "purple", "5" = "orange", "6" = "yellow")
) +
  labs(
    title = "Population Structure of Intermediate Wheatgrass Germplasm",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(pc.percent[1], 1), "% variance)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(pc.percent[2], 1), "% variance)")
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black")
  )

ggsave("PCA_biplot_clusters.pdf", pca_plot, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

#===============================================================================
# 6. GEOGRAPHIC VISUALIZATION
#===============================================================================

# Load geographic data
bioclim_data <- read.csv("IWG_bioclim.csv")

# Merge PCA clusters with geographic coordinates
geo_data <- merge(bioclim_data, pca_with_clusters,
                  by.x = "PI_Accession",
                  by.y = "sample.id",
                  all.x = FALSE)

# Load world map
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# Create map showing cluster distribution across Eurasia
geo_plot <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world, fill = "gray95", color = "gray70", size = 0.2) +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-20, 150), ylim = c(-40, 80)) +
  geom_point(data = geo_data,
             aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude, fill = as.factor(clust)),
             shape = 21, 
             size = 3, 
             alpha = 0.8,
             color = "black",
             stroke = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(
  values = c("1" = "red", "2" = "green", "3" = "blue",
             "4" = "purple", "5" = "orange", "6" = "yellow"),
  name = "Genetic Cluster",
  labels = paste0("Cluster ", 1:6)
) +
  labs(
    title = "Geographic Distribution of Genetic Clusters",
    subtitle = "Intermediate wheatgrass native range (n = 341)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", size = 0.2),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "aliceblue")
  )

ggsave("Geographic_cluster_distribution.pdf", geo_plot, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
#===============================================================================
# 7. SPATIAL POPULATION STRUCTURE (TESS3R)
#===============================================================================

# Prepare genotype matrix for tess3r
genofile <- snpgdsOpen("IWG.gds")
geno_matrix <- snpgdsGetGeno(genofile, snp.id = snpset.id)
snpgdsClose(genofile)

# Prepare coordinate matrix (must match genotype order)
coords <- geo_data %>%
  arrange(match(PI_Accession, pca_data$sample.id)) %>%
  select(Longitude, Latitude) %>%
  as.matrix()

# Run TESS3 for K = 1 to 10
tess3_results <- list()
for (k in 1:10) {
  tess3_results[[k]] <- tess3(X = geno_matrix,
                              coord = coords,
                              K = k,
                              ploidy = 6,  # Hexaploid
                              lambda = 1.0,
                              method = "projected.ls")
}

# Calculate cross-entropy for model selection
cross_entropy <- sapply(tess3_results, function(x) x$crossentropy)

# Plot cross-entropy criterion
ce_plot <- ggplot(data.frame(K = 1:10, CE = cross_entropy), 
                  aes(x = K, y = CE)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10) +
  labs(
    title = "TESS3 Cross-Entropy Criterion",
    x = "Number of Ancestral Populations (K)",
    y = "Cross-Entropy"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)

ggsave("TESS3_crossentropy.pdf", ce_plot, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Extract Q-matrix for optimal K (K = 6, matching HCPC)
Q_matrix <- qmatrix(tess3_results[[6]])

# Create spatial interpolation plot
plot(tess3_results[[6]], coords, 
     method = "map.max",
     interpol = FieldsKrigModel(10),
     main = "Spatial Genetic Structure (K = 6)",
     xlab = "Longitude",
     ylab = "Latitude",
     resolution = c(300, 300),
     cex = 0.5)

# Export Q-matrix
write.csv(Q_matrix, "TESS3_Q_matrix_K6.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#===============================================================================
# 8. KINSHIP MATRIX CALCULATION
#===============================================================================

# Reload genotype data in numeric format for kinship calculation
genofile <- snpgdsOpen("IWG.gds")
geno_kinship <- snpgdsGetGeno(genofile, snp.id = snpset.id, snpfirst = FALSE)
sample_ids <- read.gdsn(index.gdsn(genofile, "sample.id"))
snpgdsClose(genofile)

# Transpose and prepare for rrBLUP (samples as rows, markers as columns)
geno_kinship <- t(geno_kinship)
rownames(geno_kinship) <- sample_ids

# Calculate kinship matrix using VanRaden method
# K = (M - P)(M - P)' / (2 * sum(p * (1-p)))
# where M is the marker matrix and P is 2*allele frequency
K_matrix <- A.mat(geno_kinship, 
                  min.MAF = NULL,  # Already filtered
                  max.missing = 1,  # Already filtered
                  impute.method = "mean",
                  return.imputed = FALSE)

# Examine kinship distribution
kinship_values <- K_matrix[lower.tri(K_matrix)]
cat("\nKinship matrix summary:\n")
cat("Mean relatedness:", round(mean(kinship_values), 4), "\n")
cat("Range:", round(min(kinship_values), 4), "to", round(max(kinship_values), 4), "\n")

# Export kinship matrix
write.csv(K_matrix, "Kinship_matrix_VanRaden.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# Visualize kinship matrix (heatmap)
pdf("Kinship_matrix_heatmap.pdf", width = 10, height = 10)
heatmap(K_matrix, 
        col = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(100),
        symm = TRUE,
        main = "Kinship Matrix Heatmap",
        cexRow = 0.3,
        cexCol = 0.3)
dev.off()

#===============================================================================
# 9. EXPORT COVARIATES FOR GWAS
#===============================================================================

# Prepare final covariate file with:
# - Sample IDs
# - First 5 PCs (explaining ~20% of genetic variance)
# - Cluster assignments
# - Q-matrix from TESS3

gwas_covariates <- data.frame(
  sample.id = pca_data$sample.id,
  PC1 = pca_data$EV1,
  PC2 = pca_data$EV2,
  PC3 = pca_data$EV3,
  PC4 = pca_data$EV4,
  PC5 = pca_data$EV5,
  Cluster = pca_with_clusters$clust,
  Q_matrix
)

write.csv(gwas_covariates, "GWAS_population_structure_covariates.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n=================================================================\n")
cat("Population structure analysis complete!\n")
cat("=================================================================\n")
cat("\nFiles generated:\n")
cat("1. IWG_pca_eigenvectors.csv - PCA results\n")
cat("2. IWG_pca_with_clusters.csv - PCA with cluster assignments\n")
cat("3. PCA_scree_plot.pdf - Variance explained by PCs\n")
cat("4. HCPC_dendrogram.pdf - Hierarchical clustering tree\n")
cat("5. PCA_biplot_clusters.pdf - 2D PCA plot with clusters\n")
cat("6. Geographic_cluster_distribution.pdf - Map of cluster locations\n")
cat("7. TESS3_crossentropy.pdf - Model selection plot\n")
cat("8. TESS3_Q_matrix_K6.csv - Ancestry proportions\n")
cat("9. Kinship_matrix_VanRaden.csv - Relatedness matrix\n")
cat("10. Kinship_matrix_heatmap.pdf - Kinship visualization\n")
cat("11. GWAS_population_structure_covariates.csv - Final covariates\n")
cat("\n=================================================================\n")

#===============================================================================
# END OF SCRIPT
#===============================================================================
```
